headline: Marshall Islands FIRST BASELINE CONFIRM at 39.1 / Developing — climate-vulnerability + US nuclear-testing-legacy small atoll state. 1946-1958 67 US nuclear tests on Bikini and Enewetak Atolls (including 1954 Castle Bravo 15-megaton thermonuclear test — 1,000x the first Bikini tests) — direct EMP/historical-harm dimension with ongoing radiation poisoning, elevated cancer rates, birth defects, food/water contamination. 2024 Compact of Free Association with US (20 years, $50M annually beginning FY2024; $200M over 20 years joint healthcare; climate adaptation funding). Runit Dome (Enewetak Atoll) nuclear waste disposal site climate-change impact study mandated by NDAA FY2022. Active advocacy: 'urging US to better address climate change and its nuclear legacy.' Published 39.1 (Developing) plausible — climate-vulnerability + nuclear-legacy structural context constrains upward movement despite active advocacy. No major in-window events.
boundaryCase_note: Composite 39.1 is within 1.0 of Functional floor (40.0); boundary case flagged. Structural nuclear-legacy harm constrains EMP upward movement despite active state advocacy on accountability.
key_evidence: 1946-1958: 67 US nuclear tests on Bikini and Enewetak Atolls.
1954 Castle Bravo: 15-megaton thermonuclear test — 1,000x the force of first Bikini tests; largest US thermonuclear test.
Ongoing nuclear-legacy harms: radiation poisoning, elevated cancer rates, birth defects, food/water contamination — persists to present.

January 2024: New Compact of Free Association MOU signed with US (20 years). Key terms: $50M annually beginning FY2024; $200M over 20 years for joint health care and joint strategic health initiative; technical assistance for climate impacts on low-lying islands.
NDAA FY2022 mandated DOE study on climate change impacts on Runit Dome nuclear waste disposal site at Enewetak Atoll — climate-nuclear intersection risk.
Active state advocacy: urging US to better address climate change and its nuclear legacy (NBC News reporting; UN engagement).
DOE Marshall Islands Program operational — institutional engagement framework.
Greenpeace and Maryknoll OGC advocacy: structural recognition of nuclear testing harm with active rights-advocacy.
Climate-vulnerability structural context: low-lying atoll state.
No major in-window (May 9-10) compassion-relevant events identified.

evidence.source: NBC News — Marshall Islands urges U.S. to better address climate change and its nuclear legacy
evidence.finding: State-level active advocacy on climate + nuclear-legacy accountability in international forum.

evidence.source: Arms Control Association — U.S. Marshall Islands Sign Deal on Nuclear Testing Impacts
evidence.finding: 2024 Compact MOU: $50M annually; $200M 20-year healthcare; climate adaptation funding — partial accountability framework.
US institutional engagement framework operational for nuclear-legacy management.

evidence.source: Greenpeace — Legacy of US nuclear weapons tests in the Marshall Islands created global radiation exposure
evidence.finding: International civil society advocacy documenting global radiation exposure footprint of Bikini/Enewetak tests.

evidence.source: Marshallese Educational Initiative — Nuclear Testing Legacy
evidence.finding: Marshallese-led documentation of nuclear-legacy harm and accountability framework.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 sustained at published. A1 detection: state-level advocacy on nuclear-legacy + climate; documented harm acknowledgment; A4 amplification: international forum engagement. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained at published. E1 affective resonance: state-level acknowledgment of nuclear-legacy harm to population; E2 protected scope: Compact health framework targets nuclear-affected population. Anchor 3 sustained — distinctive due to historical-harm-acknowledgment posture.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 sustained at published. AC1 responsiveness: state response to nuclear legacy within constraint of US-relationship structural dependency; AC4 resource mobilization: $50M annual + $200M 20-year healthcare via Compact. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained at published. EQ1 universality: nuclear-affected population coverage via Compact; EQ2 protected categories: indigenous population rights framework; SOGI dimensions limited per regional context. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 sustained at published. B5 multilateral engagement: UN climate forum advocacy; B4 refusal ethics: declining further US testing while maintaining Compact framework; B3 scope clarity: small-state capacity. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 sustained at published. AB4 accountability-to-external-norms: active UN engagement on nuclear-legacy accountability; AB1 harm-acknowledgment: state has acknowledged nuclear-affected population needs; partial-accountability pattern. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 sustained at published. S2 long-term-impact: 20-year Compact framework + DOE Marshall Islands Program = long-term institutional structure; S5 coalitional compassion: international civil society partnership on nuclear-legacy. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 sustained at published. I1 consistency: declared nuclear-justice advocacy + Compact participation = values-action engagement within structural-dependency constraints; I2 non-performance: low-reputational-cost institutional actions sustained. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 39.1 = published. Sub-threshold drift (0.0). Developing band sustained. BOUNDARY CASE: 0.9 below Functional floor (40.0) — flagged for human-reviewer awareness.

negativeSignals: 67 US nuclear tests legacy (Bikini, Enewetak) with ongoing radiation harm, cancer, birth defects, food/water contamination; Runit Dome climate-change risk to nuclear waste containment; structural dependency on Compact framework with US; climate-existential vulnerability; small-state institutional-capacity limits; SOGI rights remain limited per regional context.
positiveSignals: Active state advocacy on nuclear-legacy + climate accountability in UN/international forums; 2024 Compact MOU with $50M annual + $200M 20-year healthcare + climate funding; DOE Marshall Islands Program institutional engagement; Marshallese-led civil society advocacy; Greenpeace and international civil society partnership; documented historical-harm acknowledgment.
sector_context: Pacific Island States / Atoll States cluster. Marshall Islands shares the climate-existential pattern with Tuvalu and Kiribati AND carries the nuclear-legacy structural context — distinctive among Pacific small island states. Cross-reference: Tuvalu, Kiribati (similar climate-vulnerability cluster), Micronesia (similar Compact/free-association cluster).
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with nuclear-legacy + climate-vulnerability as primary structural anchors
Active state advocacy on nuclear-justice and climate accountability documented
Boundary case (39.1, within 1.0 of Functional floor) flagged for human-reviewer awareness
open_actions_carried: Watch: Compact implementation milestones (annual funding delivery)
Watch: DOE Runit Dome climate-impact study outcomes
Watch: any further US nuclear-legacy accountability commitments
Watch: UN climate forum advocacy outcomes
